hooks.command: echo '## Session Context'; cat data/sessions/handoff.md 2>/dev/null || echo 'No previous session state found.'; echo '---'; echo '## Recent Git Activity'; git log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null || echo 'Not a git repo'; echo '---'; echo '## Recent Dev Activity'; tail -7 data/sessions/activity-log.md 2>/dev/null || echo 'No activity log yet.'

hooks.prompt: Before stopping: 1) Update data/sessions/handoff.md with: what was built/fixed, key decisions, current state of server/frontend, what to do next. Keep under 80 lines. 2) Append ONE summary row to data/sessions/activity-log.md. Format: | YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM | agent-name (e.g. backend-builder, frontend-builder, lead) | verb phrase (e.g. Added CRM endpoint, Fixed SSE streaming, Refactored dashboard service) | 1-line detail of what changed and why | next action or — |. NEVER write 'unknown' or 'file-write' — describe the actual work done this session. If multiple agents worked, write one row per agent. 3) If any architectural or product decisions were made, append to data/sessions/decisions.md with: | # | date | decision | rationale | active |.

Context is about to be compacted. Save current working state to data/sessions/handoff.md NOW — include: current feature/bug, files being edited, test status, approval queue state. This is your lifeline for after compaction.

hooks.command: file="$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH"; case "$file" in *.env|*.env.*|*credentials*|*secret*) echo 'BLOCKED: Cannot write to sensitive files' >&2; exit 2;; esac
file="$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH"; if [[ "$file" == *.js ]]; then node -c "$file" 2>&1 && echo 'Syntax OK' || echo 'WARNING: JavaScript syntax error detected'; fi
